subsystem: TensorFlow convolution-gradient shape inference. The function ConvBackpropComputeDimensionsV2 is a helper in the TensorFlow core kernels that, given the shapes of the forward-conv input, filter, and out_backprop tensors plus the stride/dilation/padding attributes of a Conv*BackpropInput / Conv*BackpropFilter op, validates those shapes and populates a ConvBackpropDimensions struct (batch size, in_depth, out_depth, and per-spatial-dimension geometry) that the gradient kernels use to size their loops and buffers.
provenance: Identified from the TensorFlow-specific symbols: the function name ConvBackpropComputeDimensionsV2 and ConvBackpropExtractAndVerifyDimension, the ConvBackpropDimensions / TensorShape / TensorFormat types, gtl::ArraySlice, absl::Span, StringPiece, the errors::InvalidArgument / Status::OK() / TF_RETURN_IF_ERROR / VLOG idioms, and GetTensorBatchDimIndex / GetTensorFeatureDimIndex / GetTensorSpatialDimIndex. These live in tensorflow/core/kernels (conv_grad_shape_utils.cc / conv_grad_ops). The banner comment `/* sample: primevul_validation_0073_fixed */` marks this as the PATCHED revision of a PrimeVul sample, and the filename token cwe369 points at a division/modulo-by-zero that this revision guards.
trust_boundary: This runs inside a TensorFlow op kernel at graph-execution time. Every argument is attacker-influenceable by whoever supplies the model/graph and its input tensors: num_spatial_dims, data_format, padding, and explicit_paddings come from op attributes on a possibly-malicious GraphDef/SavedModel; input_shape, filter_shape, and out_backprop_shape come from tensors whose ranks and dimension sizes an attacker can set arbitrarily (including 0-sized or degenerate dims). A hostile model can therefore drive every shape/index/arithmetic path here. The impact class for TensorFlow shape-inference bugs is denial of service (crash of the serving process) and occasionally out-of-bounds memory access; these are the CVE-generating surface for TF.

what_the_code_does.explanation: The signature. `label` is a StringPiece used only for building error messages. `num_spatial_dims` is the number of convolved spatial axes (1 for Conv1D, 2 for Conv2D, 3 for Conv3D). `input_shape`, `filter_shape`, and `out_backprop_shape` are the three tensor shapes whose consistency is being verified. `dilations` and `strides` are the per-axis dilation/stride attributes. `padding` is an enum (SAME/VALID/EXPLICIT). `explicit_paddings` is an absl::Span of int64 that, when padding==EXPLICIT, carries two entries (before,after) per tensor dimension. `data_format` selects the physical dimension ordering (e.g. NHWC vs NCHW). `dims` is the OUTPUT struct that this function fills in and that downstream gradient kernels rely on for buffer sizing. Every one of these is derived from the untrusted graph/tensors.

`num_dims` is computed as `num_spatial_dims + 2`, adding the batch and feature (channel) axes to the spatial axes. This single `int` value becomes the assumed rank of all three tensors and is used later to compute dimension indices. The first guard rejects the call if the input tensor's rank (`input_shape.dims()`) does not equal `num_dims`. Note the arithmetic `num_spatial_dims + 2` is performed in signed `int` with no bound on `num_spatial_dims`.

The same rank check is repeated for the filter tensor and the out_backprop (upstream gradient) tensor: both must also have exactly `num_dims` dimensions or the call is rejected with InvalidArgument. After these three gates, the code assumes all three shapes are rank-`num_dims`, so subsequent `dim_size(...)` indexing with indices in [0, num_dims) is in range for those shapes.

`GetTensorBatchDimIndex` maps (num_dims, data_format) to the physical index of the batch axis. That index is used to read `input_shape.dim_size(batch_dim)` into `dims->batch_size`, and then the same batch axis of out_backprop must match, otherwise InvalidArgument. This establishes the batch dimension of the output dims struct. Correctness depends on GetTensorBatchDimIndex returning an index in [0, num_dims) for the given data_format.

The feature (channel) axis index is computed, and `dims->in_depth` is read from that axis of the input. The filter's input-depth is at index `num_dims - 2` (second-to-last filter dim). This block is the heart of the CWE-369 fix: line 38 rejects the call when `filter_shape.dim_size(num_dims - 2) <= 0`, i.e. a zero or negative filter depth. That guard is what makes the very next statement safe: line 42 computes `dims->in_depth % filter_shape.dim_size(num_dims - 2)` to require the input depth be an exact multiple of the filter depth (grouped-convolution constraint). Because line 38 already rejected a zero divisor, the modulo can no longer divide by zero. The `>= 0`-was-loosened-to-`<= 0` guard also excludes negative sizes.

`dims->out_depth` is the last filter dimension (index num_dims-1), i.e. the number of output channels. It must equal the feature-axis size of out_backprop or the call is rejected. This ties the gradient tensor's channel count to the filter's output-channel count.

The per-spatial-axis loop. `dims->spatial_dims` is resized to `num_spatial_dims` entries. For each spatial axis i, `GetTensorSpatialDimIndex` maps it to a physical tensor index `image_dim`. When padding is EXPLICIT, the before/after pad values are read from `explicit_paddings` at indices `2 * image_dim` and `2 * image_dim + 1`. Then ConvBackpropExtractAndVerifyDimension is called to compute and validate that axis's output size, dilation, stride, and effective filter size, writing into `dims->spatial_dims[i]`; TF_RETURN_IF_ERROR propagates any failure. This is where the padding span is indexed with an index derived from data_format and never checked against the span's actual length.

If every gate passed and every spatial dimension was verified, the function returns OK with `dims` fully populated. Callers treat a non-error return as license to use dims->batch_size, in_depth, out_depth, and spatial_dims to size buffers and loops in the gradient computation.

what_could_go_wrong.explanation: `explicit_paddings` is an absl::Span<const int64> whose size is NEVER validated in this function. When padding==EXPLICIT it is indexed at `2 * image_dim` and `2 * image_dim + 1`, where image_dim comes from GetTensorSpatialDimIndex(num_dims, data_format, i). operator[] on absl::Span performs no bounds check. A malicious graph can set padding=EXPLICIT while supplying an explicit_paddings attribute that is shorter than 2*num_dims (e.g. empty, or the wrong length for the chosen data_format), producing an out-of-bounds read past the span's backing store. The invariant the implementation must uphold — and which is enforced elsewhere in the correct kernels but is absent here — is that explicit_paddings.size() == 2 * num_dims (and that each index 2*image_dim+1 < size) BEFORE any element is dereferenced. Impact is out-of-bounds read: process crash / information disclosure into the pad values (CWE-125).

This is the CWE-369 divide/modulo-by-zero that the sample name flags. In the UNPATCHED code the guard at lines 38-41 is absent (or is a weaker `< 0` test), so a filter tensor whose input-depth dimension (index num_dims-2) is 0 flows directly into `dims->in_depth % filter_shape.dim_size(num_dims - 2)` at line 42 — an integer modulo by zero, which is undefined behavior and aborts the TensorFlow process (denial of service) on any attacker-crafted zero-depth filter. In THIS patched revision the risk is mitigated: line 38 rejects any filter depth `<= 0` before the modulo executes, so the divisor is provably >= 1. The residual contract to confirm is that dim_size returns a signed int64 and that no other caller reaches line 42 with a zero divisor. The finding is reported so the student learns the class, but note it is guarded here.

`num_spatial_dims` is a signed int taken from the op and is used as `spatial_dims.resize(num_spatial_dims)`. std::vector::resize takes a size_t, so a negative num_spatial_dims sign-converts to an enormous value and throws std::length_error / bad_alloc (crash). The only upstream constraint is that input_shape.dims() must equal num_spatial_dims + 2 (line 10): an attacker who supplies a rank-1 input together with num_spatial_dims = -1 satisfies dims()==num_dims==1 and slips through to resize(-1). The safe invariant is that num_spatial_dims is validated to be a small non-negative value (TF restricts it to 1..3) before it is used as a size or loop bound. Impact: denial of service (CWE-190 / CWE-681 signed-to-unsigned conversion).

what_could_go_wrong.code:
const int num_dims = num_spatial_dims + 2;
  if (input_shape.dims() != num_dims) {
what_could_go_wrong.explanation: `num_dims = num_spatial_dims + 2` is signed int addition with no upper bound on num_spatial_dims. If num_spatial_dims is near INT_MAX the addition overflows (signed-overflow UB), and num_dims can become negative; it is then used as an index base for GetTensorBatchDimIndex/GetTensorFeatureDimIndex and in `num_dims - 2` / `num_dims - 1` dim_size lookups. The dims() equality check catches most mismatches, but the arithmetic itself is UB and the derived indices are trusted without an explicit `num_dims >= 3` / positive check. The implementation must clamp num_spatial_dims to a small sane range so this addition and the subsequent index math cannot overflow or go negative (CWE-190).

what_could_go_wrong.code:
int batch_dim = GetTensorBatchDimIndex(num_dims, data_format);
  dims->batch_size = input_shape.dim_size(batch_dim);
what_could_go_wrong.explanation: The dimension indices returned by GetTensorBatchDimIndex / GetTensorFeatureDimIndex / GetTensorSpatialDimIndex (lines 22, 32, 53) are trusted to be in [0, num_dims) and to be consistent with the chosen data_format. TensorShape::dim_size(index) with an out-of-range or negative index is itself a CHECK-fail/abort in TF. If an attacker-supplied data_format is inconsistent with num_dims (or num_dims is degenerate per the overflow above), these helpers can return an index that makes dim_size fault. The invariant is that (num_dims, data_format) are validated to be a supported combination before any dim_size(index) call; this function assumes it rather than checking it (CWE-20 missing validation feeding CWE-125/abort).

summary: This is the PATCHED revision of a TensorFlow conv-gradient shape helper: the modulo-by-zero (CWE-369) at line 42 is now guarded by the `filter depth <= 0` check at lines 38-41, so the headline bug is mitigated. The strongest remaining exposure is the unbounded indexing of the explicit_paddings span at lines 56-57 (out-of-bounds read, no size check), plus signed-range hazards on num_spatial_dims used for resize and num_dims arithmetic. All are DoS-class bugs reachable from an untrusted graph.

reviewed_anchors.reason: Entry point whose every parameter is attacker-influenced; carries the explicit_paddings span and num_spatial_dims that drive the findings.
Signed int used as resize() size and loop bound; negative/huge value is a DoS (lines 51-52).
Indexed at 2*image_dim / 2*image_dim+1 with no size validation -> OOB read (lines 56-57).
Feeds GetTensor*DimIndex; an inconsistent value can yield an out-of-range dim index (lines 22,32,53).

reviewed_anchors.anchor: const gtl::ArraySlice<int32>& dilations / const std::vector<int32>& strides
reviewed_anchors.reason: Only forwarded to ConvBackpropExtractAndVerifyDimension here; not indexed or size-derived in this function, so no local contract.
A tag that gates the explicit_paddings read; the risk is attributed to the span index finding, not the enum itself.
Output aggregate populated field-by-field; each field's risk is covered by the specific read that fills it.
Signed addition with no bound; overflow/negative propagates into index math (CWE-190).
These are the validation gates; they correctly reject rank mismatches. Noted, no defect.
Return trusted dim indices; an out-of-range result makes dim_size abort (CWE-20 -> CWE-125).
Pure equality comparison of two dim sizes; no arithmetic hazard.
This IS the CWE-369 fix; correctly rejects zero/negative divisor before the modulo.
Modulo-by-zero class (CWE-369); reported as the flagged bug though guarded in this revision.
Equality comparison only; no division, index, or copy hazard.
Negative num_spatial_dims sign-converts to a huge size_t -> length_error/DoS (CWE-681/190).
Delegated validator (not shown); its own bounds/overflow checks are out of scope of this file, though it is where padding_before/after are consumed.

findings.title: Out-of-bounds read of explicit_paddings span (no size check)
findings.where_it_lives: corpus/train/train_0025_cwe369.cpp (and the conv-grad kernels that call it)
findings.invariant: When padding==EXPLICIT, explicit_paddings.size() must equal 2*num_dims and every index 2*image_dim+1 must be < explicit_paddings.size() before any element is dereferenced.
findings.failure_mode: A malicious graph sets padding=EXPLICIT and supplies an explicit_paddings attribute shorter than 2*num_dims (or inconsistent with data_format). absl::Span::operator[] does no bounds check, so 2*image_dim / 2*image_dim+1 reads past the backing array — out-of-bounds read causing a crash or leaking adjacent memory into the pad values used by ConvBackpropExtractAndVerifyDimension.
findings.what_to_confirm: Confirm there is an explicit `OP_REQUIRES(explicit_paddings.size() == 2 * num_dims)` (or equivalent) before this loop; if only the caller checks it, confirm every caller does.

findings.title: Modulo by zero on filter depth (CWE-369, guarded in this revision)
findings.invariant: filter_shape.dim_size(num_dims - 2) must be strictly greater than zero before it is used as a modulo divisor.
findings.failure_mode: Without the guard, an attacker-supplied filter tensor whose input-depth dimension is 0 makes `dims->in_depth % 0` undefined behavior, aborting the TensorFlow process (DoS). In THIS patched revision the guard at lines 38-41 (`filter_shape.dim_size(num_dims - 2) <= 0` -> InvalidArgument) executes first, so the divisor is >= 1 and the bug is mitigated. Reported to teach the class and to verify the fix is not later regressed or bypassed.
findings.what_to_confirm: Confirm lines 38-41 always precede line 42 on every path, that dim_size returns a signed value so `<= 0` catches negatives, and that no alternate entry point performs the modulo without the guard.

findings.title: Negative num_spatial_dims sign-converts in vector::resize (DoS)
findings.invariant: num_spatial_dims must be validated as a small non-negative value (TF supports 1..3) before it is used as a container size or loop bound.
findings.failure_mode: num_spatial_dims is a signed int with no positivity check; a caller/graph passing a negative value together with a matching low-rank input (dims()==num_spatial_dims+2) passes the rank gate, then resize(negative) converts to an enormous size_t and throws std::length_error/bad_alloc, crashing the process.
findings.what_to_confirm: Confirm num_spatial_dims is constrained to 1..3 (or >=0) by the op registration/caller before this function is invoked.

findings.title: Unbounded signed arithmetic for num_dims feeds index math
findings.bug_class: Signed integer overflow / unchecked derived index
findings.invariant: num_spatial_dims must be small enough that num_spatial_dims + 2 cannot overflow and num_dims is guaranteed >= 3, since num_dims-1/num_dims-2 and the GetTensor*DimIndex helpers depend on it.
findings.failure_mode: With num_spatial_dims near INT_MAX the addition overflows (signed UB) and num_dims may become negative; it is then used to derive dimension indices (GetTensor*DimIndex, num_dims-2, num_dims-1) that are trusted without a positive-range check, potentially yielding out-of-range dim_size indices. Largely gated by the dims() equality checks but the arithmetic itself is UB.
findings.what_to_confirm: Confirm num_spatial_dims is range-limited before line 9 and that num_dims >= 3 is established before any num_dims-based index is used.

findings.title: Trusted dimension indices from GetTensor*DimIndex not validated against data_format
findings.bug_class: Missing input validation feeding out-of-range index
findings.invariant: (num_dims, data_format) must be a supported combination so that the returned index is always in [0, num_dims) before it reaches TensorShape::dim_size.
findings.failure_mode: An attacker-supplied data_format that is inconsistent with num_dims (or a degenerate num_dims from the overflow above) can make these helpers return an out-of-range or negative index; TensorShape::dim_size(index) then CHECK-fails/aborts, a DoS. This function assumes the helpers are always well-formed rather than validating data_format.
findings.what_to_confirm: Confirm data_format is validated (e.g. FormatFromString succeeded and matches num_dims) before these helpers are used, and that dim_size is never called with an unchecked index.

audit_checklist: Verify explicit_paddings.size() == 2*num_dims is enforced before the loop at lines 52-63 whenever padding==EXPLICIT; absl::Span operator[] is unchecked.
Confirm the filter-depth guard (lines 38-41) runs on every path before the modulo at line 42, and that dim_size is signed so `<= 0` rejects negatives — the CWE-369 fix must not regress.
Confirm num_spatial_dims is constrained to a small non-negative range (1..3) before resize(num_spatial_dims) at line 51 and before it bounds the loop.
Confirm num_spatial_dims + 2 (line 9) cannot overflow and that num_dims >= 3 is guaranteed before num_dims-2 / num_dims-1 index math.
Confirm data_format is validated against num_dims so GetTensorBatchDimIndex/FeatureDimIndex/SpatialDimIndex always return in-range indices for dim_size.
Inspect ConvBackpropExtractAndVerifyDimension for its own overflow/zero checks on strides, dilations, and padding_before/after (it consumes the possibly-OOB pad values).
